title: Global Objects Storage
text: Global Objects Storage All Uniface object definitions, including those for global objects, are stored in the Repository. When compiled, they are stored as in the location specified by the $RESOURCES_OUTPUT assignment setting. This location is then also used for testing and debugging components. Note: It is possible to compile directly to a UAR file, but this has a performance penalty and is not recommended. To optimize performance in a deployed application, compiled global objects are stored in UAR file. You can create and manipulate UAR files using the Uniface Resource Manager ( urm ). For more information, see Uniface Resource Manager (URM) . Storing global objects as resource files makes it easy to distribute applications in a modular fashion. They are visible as separate objects and can be put into different UAR files. For example, you can put the global objects belonging to a particular part of an application in the same UAR as the other components of that same application. Related Links $RESOURCES_OUTPUT Specifies the location to which Uniface compiles objects, and from which objects are read by the Uniface test environment. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
